turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="bg" x1="0" x2="0" y1="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#cfeac2" />
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#9ccf9a" />
    </linearGradient>
    <radialGradient id="fur" cx="50%" cy="30%" r="80%">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#3a3a3a" />
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#111" />
    </radialGradient>
  </defs>

  <!-- background -->
  <rect width="512" height="512" fill="url(#bg)" />
  <!-- ground shadow -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="470" rx="210" ry="24" fill="#2e4d34" opacity="0.35" />

  <!-- body and chest -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="320" rx="160" ry="140" fill="url(#fur)" />
  <!-- lighter chest patch -->
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="330" rx="90" ry="70" fill="#4f4f4f" opacity="0.35" />

  <!-- left arm (viewer right) -->
  <path d="M380 270 C 460 300, 420 420, 320 420 C 300 420, 290 380, 310 360 C 340 330, 360 300, 380 270 Z" fill="#1f1f1f" />
  <!-- right arm (viewer left) -->
  <path d="M132 270 C 52 300, 90 420, 190 420 C 210 420, 220 380, 200 360 C 170 330, 150 300, 132 270 Z" fill="#1f1f1f" />

  <!-- fists / knuckles in front -->
  <g transform="translate(0,10)">
    <ellipse cx="200" cy="420" rx="54" ry="40" fill="#2a2a2a" />
    <ellipse cx="316" cy="420" rx="60" ry="44" fill="#2a2a2a" />
    <!-- knuckle highlights -->
    <ellipse cx="190" cy="412" rx="12" ry="8" fill="#474747" />
    <ellipse cx="210" cy="412" rx="12" ry="8" fill="#474747" />
    <ellipse cx="302" cy="412" rx="14" ry="9" fill="#474747" />
    <ellipse cx="322" cy="412" rx="14" ry="9" fill="#474747" />
  </g>

  <!-- head -->
  <g>
    <ellipse cx="256" cy="170" rx="92" ry="88" fill="#171717" />
    <!-- brow ridge -->
    <path d="M180 150 C 200 120, 300 120, 332 150 C 320 140, 280 132, 256 132 C 232 132, 192 140, 180 150 Z" fill="#0f0f0f" />
    <!-- face muzzle -->
    <ellipse cx="256" cy="200" rx="52" ry="46" fill="#6b6b6b" />
    <!-- nose area -->
    <path d="M232 196 C 236 186, 276 186, 280 196 C 276 198, 236 198, 232 196 Z" fill="#4a4a4a" />
    <!-- nostrils -->
    <ellipse cx="246" cy="202" rx="6" ry="8" fill="#262626" />
    <ellipse cx="266" cy="202" rx="6" ry="8" fill="#262626" />
    <!-- mouth shadow -->
    <path d="M240 214 C 250 222, 262 222, 272 214" stroke="#2a2a2a" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" fill="none" opacity="0.9" />

    <!-- eyes -->
    <ellipse cx="236" cy="176" rx="12" ry="9" fill="#fff" />
    <ellipse cx="276" cy="176" rx="12" ry="9" fill="#fff" />
    <circle cx="236" cy="178" r="5" fill="#0b0b0b" />
    <circle cx="276" cy="178" r="5" fill="#0b0b0b" />
    <!-- small catchlights -->
    <circle cx="233" cy="175" r="2" fill="#fff" opacity="0.9" />
    <circle cx="273" cy="175" r="2" fill="#fff" opacity="0.9" />

    <!-- ears -->
    <ellipse cx="188" cy="170" rx="18" ry="14" fill="#151515" />
    <ellipse cx="324" cy="170" rx="18" ry="14" fill="#151515" />
  </g>

  <!-- subtle chest fur texture -->
  <path d="M200 300 C 230 280, 282 280, 312 300" stroke="#131313" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round" fill="none" opacity="0.15" />

</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

**Building JSON for SVG**

I’m focusing on crafting the final JSON output. I need to compose the SVG string, ensuring that any double quotes are properly escaped for validity. It's key that the output is in correct JSON format, so I'll handle those details. I’m thinking about the SVG content itself, including a gradient background. I need to remember the rules regarding IDs and text elements, specifically that they don’t apply to IDs. I’m constructing this carefully!**Creating SVG Content**

I'm focusing on producing SVG content while keeping everything straightforward. I’m starting with the basic structure and a gradient background defined by a linear gradient. Next, I'm adding elements like rectangles and ellipses to build the body shapes, with attention to details for the chest, arms, head, and facial features.

I need to remember to remove any comments since they aren't allowed in XML. As I write my SVG, I’ll ensure to escape double quotes for JSON formatting. Let’s get going!**Preparing JSON with SVG**

I'm getting ready to produce the final JSON output with the correctly escaped SVG content. I need to watch out for any illegal characters and remember that JSON requires double quotes for string boundaries. Inside those strings, double quotes will also need to be escaped. My plan is to create a long string for the JSON value while preserving newlines for clarity. I think I’m on the right path to get this done correctly!

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

message.reasoning:
**Generating guesses for animals**

I need to create a JSON structure with an array of 20 single-word English guesses. The image shows a cartoon gorilla-like ape, so I'll start with "gorilla." Next, I’ll think about related animals like "ape," "monkey," "chimp," and "gibbon." I'll avoid using "baboon" and ensure no repeats. I wonder if "silverback" can count as one word. I’ll list the words based on my confidence, keeping variations in mind and sticking to single words!**Generating guesses for stuffed animals**

I need to come up with an array of 20 single-word guesses. The drawing looks cute and round, making me think of words like "stuffed," "toy," and "stuffedanimal." I'm not sure if "stuffedanimal" counts, but I should lean toward more common nouns. I want to include "panda" lower on my list since it fits the round shape, along with others like "hippopotamus," "bear," and "koala." I’ll organize my guesses by confidence, avoiding repeats and focusing on single words.**Finalizing the guess list**

I’m drafting my final list of 20 single-word guesses, starting with "gorilla," "ape," and "primate," and moving down to "monkey," "chimpanzee," and "silverback." I’m including "panda," "bear," and "koala," even "sloth" and "hippo," although "hippo" is less likely, given the image. I noted that "meerkat" is technically a mongoose, not a primate, but I’ll include it as an animal guess. I will double-check for repetition and ensure all terms are in EN-US. Let's finalize this!**Preparing final JSON**

I’m finalizing my list of guesses, which includes "gorilla," "ape," "chimpanzee," "chimp," "monkey," and more. I'm thinking about using "hippopotamus" instead of "hippo," even though it's longer, because the image doesn't clearly represent a hippo. Should I also consider "meerkat" or maybe "otter"? I need exactly 20 guesses in total for the final list. I'll make sure "stuffedtoy" is counted as a single word. Now, let’s prepare the JSON!
